proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, at least one feature is associated with objective_response, with response rate differing between the two levels.
Patients with feature_013=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than patients with feature_013=0.
Patients with feature_067=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than patients with feature_067=0.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: pd.crosstab + chi2_contingency; logOR each
analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 77 binary features. Top hits: feature_013 ORR 15.4% (n=32365) vs 19.6% (n=17635), logOR=-0.295, p=1.1e-33; feature_067 ORR 14.4% vs 17.7%, logOR=-0.252, p=2.0e-18; feature_006 ORR 18.2% vs 16.4%, logOR=+0.127, p=1.0e-06; feature_007 ORR 17.5% vs 16.4%, logOR=+0.083, p=5.9e-04; feature_039 ORR 17.4% vs 16.4%, logOR=+0.070, p=3.4e-03. 7 of 77 binaries reach p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous feature is linearly associated with the log-odds of objective_response.
Higher feature_011 is associated with a LOWER probability of objective_response (negative association).
Higher feature_092 is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response (positive association).

analyses.code: for c in continuous_cols: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(zscore(x)))
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic per-SD: feature_011 beta=-0.149/SD, p=1.8e-32 (NEGATIVE); feature_099 beta=+0.045/SD, p=1.4e-04 (positive); feature_063 beta=-0.044/SD, p=6.9e-04 (negative); feature_092 beta=+0.035/SD, p=3.2e-03 (positive). 4 of 36 continuous features reach p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (3-level ordinal 0/1/2) shows a monotonically DECREASING objective_response rate as level increases.
Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response rate.
Smoking status (feature_057: current/former/never) is associated with objective_response rate.
Race/ethnicity (feature_123) is associated with objective_response rate.
Insurance status (feature_005) is associated with objective_response rate.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_051')['objective_response'].mean(); sm.Logit linear-trend
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic trend: feature_051=0 ORR 21.1% (n=17557), =1 ORR 15.7% (n=24962), =2 ORR 11.1% (n=7481). Linear-trend logistic beta_per_SD=-0.254, p=1.7e-93. Suggests feature_051 may represent ECOG performance status or similar ordinal prognostic indicator.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_043'], y))
analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma ORR 16.7% vs squamous 17.4%; chi-square p=0.078 (not significant).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_057'], y))
analyses.result_summary: Current 17.2%, former 16.8%, never 16.4%; chi-square p=0.27 (not significant).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_123'], y))
analyses.result_summary: ORR by race: white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, black 17.8%, asian 17.7%, other 18.5%. Chi-square p=0.045 (borderline). White patients have the lowest unadjusted ORR.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], y))
analyses.result_summary: Medicaid 16.1%, medicare 16.8%, private 17.2%, uninsured 17.0%; chi-square p=0.23 (not significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all top univariate predictors (feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_011, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_051, feature_033) are entered together in a multivariable logistic model, each retains an independent significant association with objective_response in the same direction as its univariate effect.
After adjustment for the top predictors, race/ethnicity (feature_123) and insurance (feature_005) remain associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X = main effects + race + insurance + smoking + histology dummies)
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (n=50000, pseudo-R^2=0.020). Independent predictors confirmed: f013 OR=0.74 p=1.2e-34; f067 OR=0.77 p=5.1e-19; f011 OR=0.86/SD p=1.7e-33; f051 OR=0.77/SD p=1.2e-95; f006 OR=1.14 p=1.7e-06; f099 OR=1.05/SD p=1.1e-04; f063 OR=0.96/SD p=4.9e-04; f007 OR=1.08 p=8.5e-04; f039 OR=1.08 p=1.4e-03; f092 OR=1.04/SD p=2.2e-03; f033 OR=0.98/SD p=0.046. After adjustment, race/insurance/histology/smoking are NOT significant (private vs medicaid p=0.02 marginal).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one pairwise interaction between top predictors that is statistically significant after Bonferroni correction (45 tests, alpha 0.0011).
feature_092 interacts POSITIVELY with feature_006 (and similarly with feature_007 and feature_039), such that the marginal effect of these binary features is larger when feature_092 is higher.

analyses.code: for (a,b) in combinations(top10, 2): logit with interaction; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise interaction LR tests on standardized covariates. Top hits all involve feature_092: f007*f092 coef=+0.130 p=6.9e-08; f039*f092 coef=+0.126 p=1.6e-07; f006*f092 coef=+0.126 p=1.6e-06; f006*f039 coef=+0.176 p=8.3e-04; f007*f039 coef=+0.146 p=2.6e-03; f006*f007 coef=+0.157 p=2.9e-03. Six interactions reach p<0.01 (4 pass Bonferroni). All POSITIVE: features 006/007/039 act synergistically with each other and with biomarker feature_092.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The interaction between feature_092 and the binary features 006/007/039 is concentrated in the upper range of feature_092 (e.g., the top quartile), consistent with a continuous biomarker with a clinically meaningful threshold.
feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 are not mutually exclusive (they can be co-administered) and are uncorrelated with each other in the cohort.

analyses.code: df['f092_q'] = pd.qcut(df['feature_092'],4); df.groupby(['f092_q', trt])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Stratifying ORR by feature_092 quartile and treatment: for feature_006, treated ORR is 17.1%/17.1%/17.4%/21.1% in Q1-Q4 (untreated flat at 16.2-16.6%); for feature_007 the treated-untreated gap appears only in Q4 (20.4% vs 15.6%); for feature_039 same Q4-only pattern (20.1% vs 15.4%). The treatment-biomarker synergy is concentrated in the highest biomarker quartile (cutpoint ~0.47).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on each pair; phi-correlation
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise chi-square: f006 vs f007 p=0.31, phi=0.005; f006 vs f039 p=0.70, phi=-0.002; f007 vs f039 p=0.95, phi=0.000. The three binary features are mutually independent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_092 is binarized at a high threshold (>=0.50), the interaction with each of feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 becomes more pronounced than at low thresholds, with treatment effect emerging only in the high-biomarker subgroup.

analyses.code: for trt in [006,007,039]: logit(y ~ trt + hi + trt*hi) at each thresh; LR test
analyses.result_summary: At feature_092 >= 0.50: f006*hi coef=+0.343 p=1.7e-08; f007*hi coef=+0.356 p=4.5e-10; f039*hi coef=+0.376 p=4.4e-11. ORR table: in biomarker-low patients all three treatments have flat ORR ~16-17% regardless of receipt; in biomarker-high (>=0.50) ORR jumps from ~16% off-treatment to 20.9-22.5% on each treatment. At threshold 0.25 the interaction is much weaker (p=0.005-0.06); at threshold >0 it disappears (p>0.19). Threshold ~0.50 best captures the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The biomarker-treatment interaction (feature_092_high × feature_006/007/039) is itself modified by histology (feature_043), with squamous patients deriving a different benefit than adenocarcinoma patients.

analyses.code: for trt: logit(y ~ sq + hi + trt + sq*hi + sq*trt + hi*trt + sq*hi*trt); LR on 3-way
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction (squamous × biomarker_high × treatment): f006 coef=+0.153 p=0.24; f007 coef=-0.105 p=0.40; f039 coef=+0.091 p=0.47. NO significant 3-way interaction. The biomarker-driven treatment benefit is observed in BOTH histologic subtypes (adenocarcinoma: ORR 21.8% on f006/biomarker-high vs 16.4% off; squamous: ORR 23.8% vs 16.8%) -- magnitudes are similar.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The biomarker-treatment effect is modified by feature_051 (ordinal performance status surrogate): patients with worse f051 derive a smaller benefit from treatment.
The biomarker-treatment effect is modified by smoking status (feature_057), with never-smokers deriving a smaller benefit than current/former smokers.

analyses.code: in biomarker-high subset, for trt: logit(y ~ f051 + trt + f051*trt)
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker-high (n=10749), feature_051 × treatment interactions: f006 coef=+0.095 p=0.080; f007 coef=+0.123 p=0.018; f039 coef=+0.059 p=0.26. Borderline only -- absolute ORR rates by f051 stratum show comparable benefit across f051 levels. Worse-PS patients do NOT clearly lose treatment benefit.

analyses.code: in biomarker-high subset stratify by feature_057 × treatment
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker-high, treatment-vs-no-treatment ORR difference for f006: current +6.1%, former +5.1%, never +9.8%; for f007: current +6.6%, former +5.5%, never +1.9% (small n=706); for f039: current +10.0%, former +3.5%, never +2.8%. Smoking does not consistently modify benefit; if anything, current smokers may derive somewhat larger benefit from f039.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable logit including biomarker_high × treatment interactions, feature_006/007/039 main effects shrink toward zero while the interaction terms remain large and significant, demonstrating that benefit is essentially confined to biomarker-high patients.
feature_011 has a non-linear (concave) association with log-odds objective_response, with marginal effect attenuating at high feature_011 values.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X) with f013, f067, f006, f007, f039, f011, f099, f063, f092, f051, f092_hi, three biomarker*trt interactions, squamous
analyses.result_summary: Full model: f006_x_hi coef=+0.356 p=5.8e-09; f007_x_hi coef=+0.368 p=2.1e-10; f039_x_hi coef=+0.373 p=1.3e-10. Main effects of f006/f007/f039 shrink to non-significant (coefs near 0). f092_hi main effect is NEGATIVE (-0.36 p<0.001) -- biomarker-high is prognostic for WORSE response in absence of treatment, but treatment fully reverses it.

analyses.code: compare linear feature_011 vs quartile dummies with LR test
analyses.result_summary: Quartile-based binning yielded only 3 distinct quantile bins because feature_011 has 28% zeros; LR test was uninformative (negative LR statistic from collinearity). Quartile dummies showed coefficient -0.146 (Q2) and -0.321 (Q3) suggesting roughly linear-on-log-odds trend with possibly mild non-linearity. Linear specification adequate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all 77 binary features, only feature_006/007/039 show significant interaction with feature_092_high after Bonferroni correction (alpha 0.05/77 = 6.5e-04).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: logit(y ~ hi + c + hi*c); collect interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Genome-wide-style scan across all 77 binaries for biomarker_high × binary interaction. Only f006 (p=1.7e-08), f007 (p=4.5e-10), f039 (p=4.4e-11) survive Bonferroni. Next strongest is feature_115 (p=0.026), feature_109 (p=0.028) -- not significant after correction. Confirms that the biomarker-driven response signal is specific to features 006/007/039.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within biomarker-high patients, the effects of feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 are super-additive: patients receiving all three have an ORR substantially exceeding what additive single-agent effects would predict.

analyses.code: in biomarker-high: tab by 8-way combo of (006,007,039); logit with pairwise interactions
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker-high (n=10749), ORR by treatment combo (006|007|039): 0|0|0=15.5% (n=2130); 0|0|1=16.2%; 0|1|0=15.7%; 0|1|1=18.7%; 1|0|0=14.7%; 1|0|1=16.9%; 1|1|0=16.5%; 1|1|1=44.8% (n=712). Pairwise treatment interactions in biomarker-high: f006*f007 +0.747 p<0.001; f006*f039 +0.677 p<0.001; f007*f039 +0.503 p<0.001. The TRIPLET combination produces a striking ~3x ORR jump that single agents and pairs do not.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant 3-way interaction feature_006 × feature_007 × feature_039 in the full cohort, with the triple-positive cell having ORR exceeding additive predictions.
There is a significant 4-way interaction feature_006 × feature_007 × feature_039 × feature_092_high, indicating that the triple-combination benefit is itself dependent on biomarker status.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ f006+f007+f039 + all 2-way + 3-way); LR vs no 3-way
analyses.result_summary: Full-cohort 3-way interaction f006*f007*f039 coef=+0.391, LR p=1.8e-04 (significant). Even averaged across biomarker status, the triplet shows supra-additive benefit.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ f006+f007+f039+hi + all 2,3,4-way); LR vs no 4-way
analyses.result_summary: 4-way interaction f006*f007*f039*biomarker_high: coef=+1.004, LR p=6.5e-05. Cross-tab: ORR among triple-positive biomarker-high patients = 44.8% (n=712); among triple-positive biomarker-low patients = 16.8% (n=2504); among biomarker-high patients NOT receiving the triplet = 16.3% (n=10037); among biomarker-low patients NOT receiving triplet = 16.5%. The triple-combination benefit is essentially confined to biomarker-high patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the triple combination (feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1) differs by race/ethnicity, suggesting potential treatment-allocation disparity.
Receipt of the triple combination differs by insurance status.
Among biomarker-high patients receiving the triple combination, ORR differs by race/ethnicity.

analyses.code: chi2 on (race, triple)
analyses.result_summary: Triple-combo receipt by race: white 6.49%, black 6.21%, hispanic 6.37%, asian 6.58%, other 5.82%; chi-square p=0.83. NO disparity in treatment allocation by race.

analyses.code: chi2 on (insurance, triple)
analyses.result_summary: Triple-combo receipt by insurance: medicaid 6.57%, medicare 6.70%, private 6.10%, uninsured 6.45%; chi-square p=0.097. No significant disparity.

analyses.code: race × triple × hi 3-way interaction
analyses.result_summary: In the triple-combo + biomarker-high subgroup (n=712), ORR by race: asian 48.6% (n=35), black 45.5% (n=88), white 45.5% (n=492), hispanic 39.5% (n=86), other 36.4% (n=11). White vs non-white 3-way interaction with triple+biomarker: coef=+0.36 LR p=0.080 (borderline, not significant after multiple testing). No definitive evidence of race-based effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative prognostic features (feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_051) are mutually independent (low pairwise correlation), each contributing additively to lower probability of objective_response.
Negative prognostic features (e.g., feature_013) attenuate the triple-combination + biomarker-high benefit (3-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise correlations all <|0.01|. Joint logit (n=50000): f011 OR=0.86/SD p=2.6e-33; f013 OR=0.74 p=1.2e-34; f067 OR=0.77 p=5.1e-19; f051 OR=0.77/SD p=1.1e-95. All four are independent predictors; coefficients essentially identical to univariate, confirming non-overlap.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ v + tr + hi + all 2/3-way) for v in {f013, f067}
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 × triple × biomarker_hi 3-way coef=-0.247 p=0.22; feature_067 × triple × biomarker_hi coef=+0.076 p=0.74. NEITHER negative prognostic feature significantly modifies the triple-combo benefit. Stratified ORR: f013=0+triple+hi 49.6%, f013=1+triple+hi 42.3% -- both still markedly elevated above ~16% baseline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-combination + biomarker-high effect is modified by age (feature_078): older patients derive less benefit.
The triple-combination + biomarker-high effect is modified by feature_051 (PS surrogate): worse-PS patients derive less benefit.

analyses.code: for modifier in [age, f051, hist, smoking, f011, f013, f067, f099]: logit with full 3-way interaction; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Effect-modifier scan on (triple × biomarker_high). 3-way LR p-values: age 0.87; f051 0.043; squamous 0.87; never_smoker 0.60; former_smoker 0.14; f011 0.93; f013 0.22; f067 0.74; f099 0.68. Only f051 borderline -- coef +0.20 means HIGHER f051 (worse PS surrogate) MORE benefit, opposite to expectation; not Bonferroni-significant. Within triple+biomarker-high subgroup, ORR by f051: 0=46.7%, 1=43.7%, 2=43.6% -- nearly flat. Triple-combo benefit is robust across age, ECOG-surrogate, histology and smoking.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A consolidated multivariable logistic model with prognostic factors (f013, f067, f011, f051, f099, f063), individual treatments, biomarker indicator, and the triple×biomarker interaction will show: (i) all six prognostic factors retain significance, (ii) the triple×biomarker interaction term is the dominant treatment-effect estimate (OR>3), and (iii) the predicted ORR for triple+biomarker-high exceeds 0.40.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X with f013, f067, f011, f051, f099, f063, f006, f007, f039, f092_hi, triple, triple*hi)
analyses.result_summary: Final model (n=50000, pseudo-R^2=0.025). Significant coefficients: f013 OR=0.74 p=2.8e-35; f067 OR=0.77 p=5.1e-19; f011_z OR=0.86 p=2.6e-33; f051_z OR=0.77 p=1.5e-95; f099_z OR=1.05 p=9.2e-05; f063_z OR=0.95 p=2.8e-04; triple*biomarker_hi OR=4.17 p=1.3e-47. Individual f006/f007/f039 main effects, f092_hi main effect, and triple main effect are all NS. Predicted ORR (mean covariates): no treatment+biomarker-low 15.5%; no treatment+biomarker-high 15.4%; single-agent f006+biomarker-high 15.9%; triple+biomarker-low 16.3%; triple+biomarker-high 44.6%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-combination + biomarker effect strengthens monotonically as the biomarker threshold is raised (i.e., higher feature_092 cutoffs select an increasingly responsive subgroup).
The triplet f006/f007/f039 is a UNIQUE high-order combination: replacing any of these with another candidate binary feature substantially weakens the biomarker-treatment interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Triple×biomarker_hi interaction OR by threshold: 0.10:1.62 p=2.9e-05; 0.20:1.82 p=1.0e-09; 0.30:2.15 p=2.1e-17; 0.40:2.40 p=1.2e-22; 0.50:4.08 p=2.9e-47; 0.60:12.15 p=1.9e-77; 0.70:8.68 p=2.5e-19. ORR jump on triple combo: at thresh 0.50 ORR rises from 16% to 45%; at thresh 0.60 ORR jumps from 16% to ~71%! Strongly monotonic dose-response of the biomarker.

analyses.code: for triplet in combinations(top binaries, 3): logit interaction with biomarker_hi
analyses.result_summary: f006|f007|f039 interaction OR with biomarker_hi = 4.07 p=2.9e-47, much stronger than next best triplets which all include only 2 of {006,007,039} plus another binary (best alternatives reach OR ~2.0-2.3, p ~1e-14 to 1e-17). The 006/007/039 triplet is uniquely maximal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Biomarker-high patients (feature_092>=0.5) are MORE LIKELY to receive the triple combination than biomarker-low patients, indicating biomarker-driven treatment selection.
Patients with worse prognostic features (high f051, f013=1) are LESS LIKELY to receive the triple combination.

analyses.code: chi2(f092_hi, triple); ttest f092 by triple
analyses.result_summary: Triple-combo receipt: biomarker-low 6.38%, biomarker-high 6.62%; chi-square p=0.37. Mean feature_092 in triple-treated 0.312 vs untreated 0.307; t-test p=0.19. NO biomarker-driven selection -- treatment assignment is independent of biomarker.

analyses.code: groupby f051, f013 -> triple rate
analyses.result_summary: Triple by f051: 0=6.52%, 1=6.44%, 2=6.22% (essentially flat). Triple by f013: 0=6.50%, 1=6.40% (flat). Triple by age band 30-55=6.14%, 55-65=6.61%, 65-75=6.28%, 75-90=6.65% (flat). Treatment allocation is independent of all prognostic factors examined -- no confounding by indication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After Bonferroni correction across all binary features, no single binary feature significantly modifies the (triple × biomarker_high) effect (i.e., the triple×biomarker benefit is universal, not gated by another binary trait).
After Bonferroni correction across all continuous features, no continuous feature other than feature_092 itself significantly modifies the (triple × biomarker_high) effect.

analyses.code: for c in 77 binary cols: logit with mod*tr*hi 3-way; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Top hits: feature_027 coef=-0.91 p=0.0062; feature_076 coef=+0.90 p=0.0099; feature_083 coef=+0.56 p=0.014; feature_122 coef=+0.58 p=0.025. NONE survives Bonferroni correction (alpha 6.5e-04). Confirms triple-combo+biomarker effect is robust across binary subgroups.

analyses.code: for c in 36 continuous cols: logit with mod*tr*hi 3-way; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_092 itself reaches Bonferroni (alpha 1.4e-03), reflecting the dichotomization. feature_064 coef=-0.28 p=0.003 (borderline). No other continuous covariate modifies the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the FOUR negative prognostic features (f011, f013, f067, f051), each is associated with lower predicted probability of objective_response in a way that is consistent with a 'general health/burden' interpretation rather than treatment-related toxicity, because their effects do NOT depend on whether the patient received the triple combination.

analyses.code: for v in [f011_z, f013, f067, f051_z]: logit(y ~ v + triple + v*triple)
analyses.result_summary: Interactions of negative prognostic features with triple combo (full cohort): f011_z*triple coef=-0.012 p=0.78; f013*triple coef=-0.06 p=0.49; f067*triple coef=+0.06 p=0.55; f051_z*triple coef=+0.04 p=0.34. NONE significant. Negative prognostic features act as roughly proportional reductions in baseline odds, independent of treatment receipt -- consistent with disease severity / general prognostic markers rather than treatment-specific resistance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted association between race (feature_123='white') and lower objective_response rate (16.5% in white vs 17-18% non-white) loses statistical significance after adjustment for prognostic factors and treatment, indicating that racial differences in raw response rate are driven by case-mix rather than independent racial effects.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X = race + ins + age + clinical + treatment terms)
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for f013, f067, f011, f051, f099, f063, treatment, and biomarker terms, race_white coef=-0.07 p=0.13 (borderline non-significant); other race categories all p>0.49. Insurance: private vs medicaid OR=1.09 p=0.04 (modest). After full adjustment, no race category retains independent association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 has a NEGATIVE prognostic effect (lower ORR) in patients NOT receiving the triple combination, while becoming a positive predictive marker once the triple combination is given -- a classic 'biomarker reversal' pattern.

analyses.code: stratify by triple receipt; logit(y ~ f092_z) within each stratum
analyses.result_summary: Within triple=0 (n=46784): feature_092 per-SD coef=+0.013, p=0.27 (essentially neutral / slightly positive). Within triple=1 (n=3216): feature_092 per-SD coef=+0.323, p=2.1e-13 (strongly POSITIVE). Hypothesized 'reversal' is partial: biomarker is roughly neutral off-treatment but becomes a strong positive predictor under triple combo. From the binarized view, biomarker_high+no_triple ORR=16.3% vs biomarker_low+no_triple 16.5% (~no difference). Final interpretation: feature_092 is mostly a PREDICTIVE biomarker (treatment-effect modifier) rather than a prognostic one.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Number-needed-to-treat analysis: in the biomarker-high subgroup, fewer than 4 patients need to be given the triple combination to gain one additional response.

analyses.code: ARR = ORR_triple_hi - ORR_no_triple_hi; NNT = 1/ARR
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker-high stratum: ORR with triple combo 44.8% (n=712), ORR without triple 16.3% (n=10037). Absolute risk increase = 28.5%; NNT = 1/0.285 = 3.5 patients (in biomarker-high subgroup) to gain one additional objective response. In biomarker-low stratum: ORR triple 16.8% vs no-triple 16.5% (ARR ~0.3%, NNT ~333) -- minimal benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Putting all findings together, the dataset displays a textbook pattern: (a) feature_092 is a continuous predictive biomarker (clinically meaningful threshold ~0.50, reminiscent of PD-L1); (b) features 006/007/039 are three independent treatments whose individual effects are modest but whose triple combination is dramatically more effective (NNT ~3.5) and only in biomarker-high patients; (c) features 011, 013, 067, 051 are four independent negative prognostic factors (likely performance status / disease burden / comorbidity surrogates) that act additively on log-odds; (d) demographics (race, insurance, smoking, histology) show small unadjusted differences but no independent effect after multivariable adjustment; (e) treatment-allocation is approximately balanced across covariates, so the observed signals are not driven by selection bias.

analyses.code: see iter17_final_model.csv
analyses.result_summary: Final synthesis (also formalized in analysis_summary.txt). The final logistic model has pseudo-R^2 = 0.025; the dominant term is the triple×biomarker_high interaction (OR 4.17, p=1.3e-47) yielding predicted ORR 44.6% in biomarker-high patients on triple combo vs 16.3% off triple combo. Main prognostic predictors all independently significant at p<1e-04 with magnitudes consistent across strata. Demographic differences in unadjusted ORR are not retained after adjustment for clinical factors and treatment.
